calibration_batch_summary.tone_assessment: Cooperative baseline. Zero RF-03 primary signals. Logistics communication functional and non-strategic in pre-escalation period. Signal concentration expected in Phase A late (hot periods 2024-08) and Phase B/C (2025-05, 2025-10). Cross-RF foundation available: RF-02 (101 flags, gatekeeping) provides context for logistics-gatekeeping compound; cat allergy manufactured pretext (RF-15 ADD-006) is the clearest anticipated RF-03+RF-02 compound.

calibration_questions.question: RF-03 is about using logistics as a weapon, not just gatekeeping access. (a) Are there recurring patterns where Melissa changed pickup/dropoff times, locations, or conditions at the last minute in ways that felt deliberate? (b) Has she ever created 'impossible' logistics — giving you information too late to respond, or setting conditions she knew you couldn't meet? (c) How does the logistics of custody exchanges typically go — is there a pattern of confrontation at pickups or dropoffs?
calibration_questions.why_asked: The RF-02 record documents 101 access denial signals. RF-03 asks about the PROCESS — does she manipulate the logistics itself to control or punish, beyond simply denying access?
calibration_questions.classification_impact: High — establishes whether RF-03 is a primary pattern (logistics-as-weapon) or primarily RF-02 with logistics components

calibration_questions.question: (a) Has Melissa ever enrolled Olivia in recurring activities (sports, classes, events) that specifically conflict with your scheduled parenting time, without discussing it with you first? (b) If yes — is there a pattern of this, or was it a one-time situation? (c) Are there activities Olivia is currently in that conflict with your time that you feel you had no input on?
calibration_questions.why_asked: Activity enrollment conflicts are a classic RF-03 mechanism — creating standing conflicts that make 'normal' visitation impossible without the other parent appearing unreasonable for objecting.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: High — if confirmed, establishes ARC-RF03-002 (Activity Enrollment Obstruction)

calibration_questions.topic: Logistical compliance tied to unrelated demands
calibration_questions.question: (a) Has Melissa ever made logistical cooperation contingent on something unrelated — e.g., 'I'll share the school schedule once you do X' or 'pickup won't work unless you agree to Y'? (b) The cat allergy scheme (RF-15 ADD-006) tied Olivia's visitation to Eli removing his cats — is that the clearest example, or are there others? (c) Any examples where she created a situation where complying with logistics meant agreeing to something else?
calibration_questions.why_asked: Conditional logistics is an RF-03 primary pattern. The cat allergy scheme is already documented as RF-15+RF-02 compound; this question identifies if there are additional standalone RF-03 conditional compliance patterns.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: High — each confirmed conditional logistics event is an RF-03 primary flag

calibration_questions.question: The triage identifies August 2024 and May 2025 as RF-03 hot periods. (a) August 2024 was the EP-0854 mega-cluster (311 messages). Was logistics manipulation part of that conflict? (b) May 2025 is around when you started using AI-guided communication. Were there specific logistical maneuvers during that transition? (c) October 2025 is also a hot period — any logistical conflicts around the time of the police report (Nov 22 2025)?
calibration_questions.why_asked: Correlating hot periods with known conflict clusters ensures RF-03 flags align with the broader behavioral record rather than being isolated incidents.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: Medium — confirms hot period timing and severity context for Phase A/B/C arc progression

addenda_proposed.title: Cat Allergy Scheme as RF-03 Compound
addenda_proposed.rule_draft: [PENDING CP2] The cat allergy/asthma documentation scheme (Melissa took Olivia to allergy specialist → documented cat allergy + asthma → required Eli to remove cats as condition of visitation) is already classified as RF-15 Category B + RF-02 primary. Add RF-03 secondary compound when the logistical obstruction mechanism is evident: denying visitation by creating a logistics condition (cat removal) that Eli could not or would not meet. Flag: RF-03 peripheral (Category: logistical compliance contingent on unrelated demands).

addenda_proposed.title: Logistics vs. Access Denial Boundary Rule
addenda_proposed.rule_draft: [PENDING CP2] RF-02 flags access DENIAL. RF-03 flags logistical MANIPULATION of that denial. When Melissa denies access: (1) simple denial = RF-02 only; (2) denial with manufactured logistical pretext = RF-02 primary + RF-03 secondary; (3) denial through impossible logistics = RF-03 primary + RF-02 secondary. Apply this boundary consistently to avoid double-counting.

post_cp2_instructions.if_no_reclassification: Confirm addenda. Initialize ARC-RF03-001 and ARC-RF03-002 at CANDIDATE. Resume B-002. Cross-reference RF-02 batch outputs at each batch for RF-03 compound candidates. Primary signal concentration expected B-006 onward (Phase A late). Cat allergy scheme area (B-025) should yield RF-03+RF-02+RF-15 triple compound.
post_cp2_instructions.signal_sourcing_note: RF-03 signals are partially embedded in RF-02 flags. Run full pass independently — do not rely solely on RF-02 cross-references. B-001 through B-005 likely zero or minimal flags. Phase A hot periods (2024-06, 2024-08, 2023-09 per RF-06 heatmap overlap) may yield RF-03 alongside financial coercion. Phase B/C visit refusal cascade area should be checked for logistical weaponization layered on access denial.
